rulings.Case Title: MILES HENRY COBB SELLAI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Court To Determine Amount Of Medi-Cal Lien
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025 Line 1. OTHER MICHELLE BAASS DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES Amended Notice Of Motion For Court To Determine Amount Of Medi-Cal Lien. Continued to February 21, 2025, on the court's motion. No later than close of business February 14, 2015, moving and opposing counsel shall file a joint signed statement identifying their efforts to stipulate to a lien amount, noting date(s) of event(s), method of communication, and who was involved; the parties shall not disclose the content of discussions. A courtesy copy of the joint statement must be provided to this department within two court days of submission for filing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further And/Or Amended Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025 Line 3. 6 - PLAINTIFF GENOA MARTELL's Amended Motion To Compel Further And/Or Amended Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One. The prevailing party must submit a separate proposed order for each motion. Plaintiff alleges that her former employer ContextLogic, Inc. dba Wish (hereinafter "Wish") misreported her income and caused adverse tax consequences. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), pars. 18-27.) Approximately four years later, defendant Qoo10 Pte. Ltd purchased some of Wish's assets and liabilities. (FAC, par. 12.) Plaintiff served form interrogatories on Qoo10 Pte. Ltd. ("Quoo10"). In response, defendant provided code-compliant substantive responses to 4.1, 9.1, 9.2, 12.2, 12.3, 12.6, 50.1, and 50.2. Defendant also noted that after a reasonable search, it had no responsive information to respond to interrogatories 12.1, 14.1, 14.2, and 17.1. Given the nature of these interrogatories, the answers comport with CCP 2030.220(c). Plaintiff's supporting declaration for this motion does not attach the form interrogatories or responses. Rather, plaintiff attached the requests for admission and Qoo10's response. Nevertheless, based on the parties' separate statements and arguments presented, the court infers that defendant's response to the form interrogatories was properly verified. The court finds that defendant's responses were code-compliant and plaintiff lacked substantial justification in bringing this motion. Plaintiff shall pay $2,000 to defendant as sanctions, payment within10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further And/Or Amended Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025 Line 3. 7 - PLAINTIFF GENOA MARTELL's Amended Motion To Compel Further And/Or Amended Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One. The prevailing party must submit a separate proposed order for each motion. Plaintiff alleges that her former employer ContextLogic, Inc. dba Wish (hereinafter "Wish") misreported her income and caused adverse tax consequences. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), pars. 18-27.) Approximately four years later, defendant Qoo10 Pte. Ltd purchased some of Wish's assets and liabilities. (FAC, par. 12.) Plaintiff served requests for admissions on defendant. In response, defendant responded that it lacked information to admit or deny the request. (CCP Section 2033.220.) Plaintiff fails to show that these responses were evasive and the motion is denied. The court finds that defendant's responses were code-compliant and plaintiff lacked substantial justification in bringing this motion. Plaintiff shall pay $2,000 for filing this motion within 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. [Defendants are attempting to "conditionally withdraw" all their motions on the understanding that they can bring the motion again and the deadline to file the motion is extended. The Court should treat this as a regular withdrawal of the motion. There is no such thing as a conditional withdrawal. The parties must stipulate to extend the timing to file a motion.] =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO STANISLAUS COUNTY**DANIEL PAUL GARRETT-STEINMAN VS. SIERRA CLUB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION TO TRANSFER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025 Line 3. 1 - DEFENDANTs SIERRA CLUB, CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY, INC., CALIFORNIA NATIVE PLANT SOCIETY,and FRIENDS OF THE RIVER's Amended MOTION TO TRANSFER. SIERRA CLUB, CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY, INC., CALIFORNIA NATIVE PLANT SOCIETY; and FRIENDS OF THE RIVER's ("Moving Defendants") motion to transfer venue under chapter 1 of Title for of the CCP is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, this action is transferred to the Superior Court of the State of California for the County of Stanislaus ("Stanislaus Court") given that all claims arise from and are directly related to Sierra Club, et al. v. Del Puerto Water District, Stanislaus County Superior Court, Case No. CV-20-005193 ("Del Puerto litigation"), a matter adjudicated in the Stanislaus Court and the Stanislaus Court is a proper venue for the claims. (See CCP section 395(a).) Plaintiff Daniel Paul Garrett-Steinman's request that this court retain jurisdiction over motions set for or to be set for hearing in February 2025, is denied. When a matter is transferred, a court surrenders jurisdiction to the receiving court. All pending matters will have to be noticed for hearing in the Stansilaus Court once the transfer is complete. Moving Defendants to pay the court fees and costs attendant to the transfer. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).
